templates.name: VMware Hypervisor
templates.description:
You can discuss this template or leave feedback on our forum https://www.zabbix.com/forum/zabbix-suggestions-and-feedback/

This template can be used in discovery as well as manually linked to a host. To use this template as manually linked to a host, attach it to the host and set manually the value of '{$VMWARE.HV.UUID}' macro.

Note: To create custom performance counter see documentation: https://www.zabbix.com/documentation/8.0/manual/vm_monitoring/vmware_keys#footnotes

Note: To get all supported counters and generate path for custom performance counter see documentation: https://www.zabbix.com/documentation/8.0/manual/appendix/items/perf_counters

Generated by official Zabbix template tool "Templator"
templates.readme:
## Overview

This template is designed for the effortless deployment of VMware ESX hypervisor monitoring and doesn't require any external scripts.

This template can be used in discovery as well as manually linked to a host.

For additional information, please see [Zabbix documentation on VM monitoring](https://www.zabbix.com/documentation/8.0/manual/vm_monitoring).

To use this template as manually linked to a host, attach it to the host and manually set the `Hypervisor UUID` host wizard configuration field.

## Setup

To use this template as manually linked to a host:
  1. Compile Zabbix server with the required options (`--with-libxml2` and `--with-libcurl`)
  2. Set the `StartVMwareCollectors` option in the Zabbix server configuration file to "1" or more
  3. Set the host wizard configuration fields required for VMware authentication: `VMware URL`, `VMware username` and `VMware password`
  4. To get the hypervisor UUID, enable access to the hypervisor via SSH and log in via SSH using a valid login and password.
  5. Run the following command and specify the UUID in the `Hypervisor UUID` host wizard configuration field:
  ```text
  vim-cmd hostsvc/hostsummary | grep uuid
  ```

Note: To enable discovery of hardware sensors of VMware hypervisors, set the host wizard configuration field `Monitoring of hardware sensors` to the selected state.

items.name: Hypervisor ping

triggers.name: VMware Hypervisor: Hypervisor is down
triggers.description: The service is unavailable or is not accepting ICMP pings.

items.name: Cluster name
items.description: Cluster name of the guest VM.

items.name: Connection state
CPU usage in percent
items.description: CPU usage as a percentage during the interval.

items.name: CPU usage
items.description: Aggregated CPU usage across all cores on the host in Hz. This is only available if the host is connected.

items.name: CPU utilization
items.description: CPU utilization as a percentage during the interval depends on power management or hyper-threading.

items.name: Datacenter name
items.description: Datacenter name of the hypervisor.

items.name: Full name
items.description: The complete product name, including the version information.

items.name: CPU frequency
items.description: The speed of the CPU cores. This is an average value if there are multiple speeds. The product of CPU frequency and the number of cores is approximately equal to the sum of the MHz for all the individual cores on the host.

items.name: CPU model
items.description: The CPU model.

items.name: CPU cores
items.description: Number of physical CPU cores on the host. Physical CPU cores are the processors contained by a CPU package.

items.name: CPU threads
items.description: Number of physical CPU threads on the host.

items.name: Total memory
items.description: The physical memory size.

items.name: Model
items.description: The system model identification.

items.name: Bios UUID
items.description: The hardware BIOS identification.

items.name: Vendor
items.description: The hardware vendor identification.

items.name: Ballooned memory
items.description: The amount of guest physical memory that is currently reclaimed through the balloon driver. Sum of all guest VMs.

items.name: Used memory
items.description: Physical memory usage on the host.

items.name: Number of bytes received
items.description: VMware hypervisor network input statistics (bytes per second).

items.name: Number of broadcasts received
items.description: VMware hypervisor network input statistics (broadcasts).

items.name: Number of dropped received packets
items.description: VMware hypervisor network input statistics (packets dropped).

items.name: Number of errors received
items.description: VMware hypervisor network input statistics (errors).

items.name: Number of bytes transmitted
items.description: VMware hypervisor network output statistics (bytes per second).

items.name: Number of broadcasts transmitted
items.description: VMware hypervisor network output statistics (broadcasts).

items.name: Number of dropped transmitted packets
items.description: VMware hypervisor network output statistics (packets dropped).

items.name: Number of errors transmitted
items.description: VMware hypervisor network output statistics (errors).

items.name: Power usage maximum allowed
items.description: Maximum allowed power usage.

items.name: Power usage
items.description: Current power usage.

items.name: Get sensors
items.description: Master item for sensor data.

items.name: Overall status

triggers.name: VMware Hypervisor: The {$VMWARE.HV.UUID} health is Red
triggers.description: One or more components in the appliance might be in an unusable status and the appliance might soon become unresponsive. Security patches might be available.

triggers.name: VMware Hypervisor: The {$VMWARE.HV.UUID} health is Yellow
triggers.description: One or more components in the appliance might soon become overloaded.

dependencies.name: VMware Hypervisor: The {$VMWARE.HV.UUID} health is Red

items.description: System uptime.

triggers.event_name: VMware Hypervisor: Hypervisor has been restarted (uptime < 10m)

items.name: Version
items.description: Dot-separated version string.

items.name: Number of guest VMs
items.description: Number of guest virtual machines.

trigger_prototypes.name: VMware Hypervisor: The multipath count has been changed
trigger_prototypes.event_name: VMware Hypervisor: The multipath count for datastore [{#DATASTORE}] has been changed
trigger_prototypes.description: The number of available datastore paths is less than registered (`{#MULTIPATH.COUNT}`).

item_prototypes.name: Average read latency of the datastore [{#DATASTORE}]
item_prototypes.description: Average amount of time for a read operation from the datastore (milliseconds).

item_prototypes.name: Average read IOPS of the datastore [{#DATASTORE}]
item_prototypes.description: Average IOPS for a read operation from the datastore.

item_prototypes.name: Free space on datastore [{#DATASTORE}] (percentage)
item_prototypes.description: VMware datastore free space (percentage from the total).

trigger_prototypes.name: VMware Hypervisor: [{#DATASTORE}]: Free space is critically low
trigger_prototypes.event_name: VMware Hypervisor: Datastore free space is critically low ([{#DATASTORE}] free space < {$VMWARE.HV.DATASTORE.SPACE.CRIT}%)
trigger_prototypes.description: Datastore free space has fallen below the critical threshold.

trigger_prototypes.name: VMware Hypervisor: [{#DATASTORE}]: Free space is low
trigger_prototypes.event_name: VMware Hypervisor: Datastore free space is low ([{#DATASTORE}] free space < {$VMWARE.HV.DATASTORE.SPACE.WARN}%)
trigger_prototypes.description: Datastore free space has fallen below the warning threshold.

dependencies.name: VMware Hypervisor: [{#DATASTORE}]: Free space is critically low

item_prototypes.name: Average write latency of the datastore [{#DATASTORE}]
item_prototypes.description: Average amount of time for a write operation to the datastore (milliseconds).

item_prototypes.name: Average write IOPS of the datastore [{#DATASTORE}]
item_prototypes.description: Average IOPS for a write operation to the datastore (milliseconds).

macros.description: The critical threshold of the datastore free space.

config.label: Critical datastore free space threshold
config.description: Critical threshold of the datastore free space. In the range from 0 to 100 inclusive.

macros.description: The warning threshold of the datastore free space.

config.label: Warning datastore free space threshold
config.description: Warning threshold of the datastore free space. In the range from 0 to 100 inclusive.

macros.description: Set "true"/"false" to enable or disable the monitoring of hardware sensors.

config.label: Monitoring of hardware sensors
config.description: Whether it is necessary to monitor of hardware sensors.

macros.description: Sets the regex string of hardware sensor names to be allowed in discovery.

config.label: Include hardware sensors filter
config.description: Regex string to include hardware sensors by sensors name.

macros.description: Sets the regex string of hardware sensor names to be ignored in discovery.

config.label: Exclude hardware sensors filter
config.description: Regex string to exclude hardware sensors by sensors name.

macros.description: VMware service (vCenter or ESX hypervisor) SDK URL (https://servername/sdk).

config.description: URL for vCenter or ESX hypervisor (https://servername/sdk).
